channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish and Litter all over Traflagar Street, 17th June
item.description:
There has been litter and leave debris all over the street - children are at the school and there is smashed glass and rubbish everywhere. Please could this be seen to. Thank you in advance

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 194,196,198, Trafalgar Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Human excrement, 17th June
item.description:
As there is no human fouling category, not sure where to post this. Someone had a non-solid number two outside 84-86 Lakedale, between the bins, and even tried to wipe. no photo for obvious reasons. Plently of flies already harvesting. If somenoe can clean this asap would be greatly appreciated
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Lakedale Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Safety risk: gap in fence, 17th June
item.description:
A fence panel is broken between the recreational field on Kings Hall Road, and the road on Kings Hall Road. Gap is large enough for young child to escape fenced field onto busy road - please fix!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kings Hall Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Fox cub, 17th June
item.description:
The dead fox cub that was in my front garden behind hedge has been removed by myself, put in a bin bag and then put in a cotton bag which I tied up because it was smelling so much and covered in flies, it was then placed in the dumped wheelie bin (has 16A written on it) on the green underneath at the side of tree by the parking bays, can this please be collected asap, thank you.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sibthorpe Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Anything ever going to happen about oldhill parking?, 17th June
item.description:
Is the council ever going to do anything about Oldhill? Illegal parking everywhere bicycles all over the pavement, litter overflowing and a general access nightmare. Do something please

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Oldhill Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped in carpark, 17th June
item.description:
Lots of rubbish in carpark

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Torrance Close, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: FLytip, 17th June
item.description:
Email received by CSC inbox 17/06/26 10:01 Fly-tipping Lat/Long - 51.409591, -0.0454009 what3words - moon.waters.ranks Nearest address - 243 Beckenham Road, Beckenham, BR3 4RP Furniture in middle of pavement dumped by flat residents for 4 weeks uncollected Construction waste dumped NEXT to bins Used toilet paper wrapped around post Can residents please get instruction or fines??? Reference - 5645348 Map link - open Google map Report history - Status: Received 15/06/2026 12:18 Furniture in middle of pavement dumped by flat residents for 4 weeks uncollected Construction waste dumped NEXT to bins Used toilet paper wrapped around post Can residents please get instruction or fines???
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 251, Beckenham Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Young tree needing maintenance, 17th June
item.description:
Ref.9571893. I did say in my report that the tree was growing. All it needed was to replace the webbing/strap that was torn apart. Was it really necessary to uproot it? It was not a safety issue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 95, Doveney Close, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass in cycle lane, 17th June
item.description:
Hi- this area is covered with glass-could it be cleared? It is meaning bikes go on the pavement to avoid it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenwich High Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture fly tips, 17th June
item.description:
Red table and 2 chairs haves been dumped on the green area in between Pensgreene court & Trenholme terrace. Please could this be collected thank .
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Trenholme Terrace, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Considerable vegetation overgrowth causing narrowing of pavement access and severe visibility issues exiting from driveway., 17th June
item.description:
Severe vegetation overgrowth is causing a dangerous narrowing of the pavement especially for pram and pushchair users. It is also dangerous due to severe visibility issues for the homeowner when exiting his driveway.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brampton Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree outside 32 Ravenscroft Rd, 17th June
item.description:
Tree is overgrown and needs pruning. Branches overhanging path and cars. The tree is leaning so the branches will get lower over time

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ravenscroft Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Deep and wide sink hole, 17th June
item.description:
Deep and wide sink hole suddenly appeared near 3 Belvedere Rd, London SE19 2HJ. About 10cm deep (deepest point) and depression overall about 1 sqm. Immediate danger to cyclists and motorcycles especially at night or in rain and depression cannot easily be seen and is unusually deep. Urgent inspection and remedial action required.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Belvedere Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Junction Salusbury and Brooksville, 17th June
item.description:
Bollard knocked down and rubble and metal left in road. Bollard removed and stored against fence to side.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Salusbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: St George's road car park previous report 9306797 and 9618814., 17th June
item.description:
The grey lockers previously reported with the fly tipping and rubbished behind the grey lockers are situated in St George's road car park. The car park is owned by Bromley Council and not on private land. Please look at the reports listed above and reconsider address the issues. Surely it is the responsibility of the council to remove fly tipping and to clear rubbish which constitutes a health and safety issue.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albemarle Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bikes obstructing footpath, 17th June
item.description:
More consideration needed from operator

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Burney Street, East Greenwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A Fly-tip Small - Up to 3 black bags problem has been found, 17th June
item.description:
A Fly-tip Small- on bed Mattress

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Albert Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag (Estates)

item.title: Fallen Tree Branch, 17th June
item.description:
I know this issue has already been reported but I want to add some further information… On Saturday 13th June around 3pm, two men with three children under 5 were on this site with a large saw cutting branches off the trees. Not suggesting they caused this damage or contributed to the branch splitting from the tree but these trees were being damaged. However, this is not the first time I have witnessed men hacking lumps off these trees. The first time I saw it I thought they worked for the council, but in hindsight I realise now that they did not, which is why I have reported this incident this time. In addition the children were throwing glass bottles off the bank & smashing them on the pavement, but that’s a different story! We have Police CCTV in Methuen Road to monitor the drug dealers, you may be able to identify the two men on that. Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Methuen Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Left near junction St Pauls Hill Wood Road causing traffic disruption for last 2 weeks, 17th June
item.description:
Vehicle causing traffic problems at a busy junction

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 281, Leesons Hill, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Public Litter bin. has gone missing, 17th June
item.description:
We (I am speaking for the residents of College Road) have had our public litter bin taken away as we are being told that 'someone' has misused it. We object to this, as the result is on this side of the road now has no bin for about a mile stretch. This means that the pavement is filled with litter, particularly as there is a chicken shop on this side. It has affected environmental safety, leading to an increase of rats, foxes, flies and another vermin thriving off the litter, chicken bones pose a high risk to dogs being taken on a walk, and some rubbish is even thrown into our front gardens. It has been pointed out that there are two bins on the other side (strangely only one block from each other), however this does little to dissuade those constantly littering (especially from the chicken shop), and does not help those with disability who struggle to cross a very busy road which could be considered a main artery. It is not acceptable that the whole road suffers and is under threat due to a consequence of one person fly tipping (or whatever the case was). Please give us our bin back!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
